_comment: Recipient email → register classification. A=Formal-Warm, B=Mid-Casual, C=Casual-Pro. Lowercase keys.

_autoclassified.partner@hypotheticalseedfund.vc: LLM classifier high: First-touch outbound to a fund partner at a seed fund with no prior thread or vault context — canonical Register A scenario.
_autoclassified.sales@realthread.com: LLM classifier high: First-touch outbound to a sales team address at an unknown company with no prior relationship or thread history.
_autoclassified.hello@threadbird.com: LLM classifier medium: Last contact was 2708 days ago (2018) and relationship score is 1, making this effectively a cold first-touch outbound despite a prior acquaintance relationship.
_autoclassified.info@underdogla.com: LLM classifier high: First-touch outbound to an organizational info address with no prior relationship signals formal-warm register.
_autoclassified.info@realthread.com: LLM classifier high: First-touch outbound to an unrecognized external contact (info@realthread.com) with no prior thread or vault match.
_autoclassified.hi@apliiq.com: LLM classifier high: hi@apliiq.com is a first-touch outbound to an unknown contact at an external company with no prior thread.
_autoclassified.vickyli@vogracecustom.com: LLM classifier high: Vicky is a first-touch external vendor contact replying formally with 'Hi Matthew' and professional sign-off, matching the A-register criteria for first-touch external contacts.
_autoclassified.agashi@stickeryou.com: LLM classifier high: Anila is a first-touch external sales contact at StickerYou with no prior relationship in vault; her formal-warm inbound ('Hi Matthew') confirms the A-register framing.
_autoclassified.cs@apliiq.com: LLM classifier high: cs@apliiq.com is a first-touch external business contact (vendor RFQ cold outreach) and Ayush's reply uses formal 'Hello Matthew' with professional customer service tone, confirming Register A appropriate.
_autoclassified.tesla@tuozhenclothing.com: LLM classifier high: First-touch external vendor contact with no prior relationship; their formal opening 'Hi Matthew' and structured RFQ response confirms this is an initial business exchange.
_autoclassified.xycy@xinyiprint.com: LLM classifier high: Cyan is a first-touch external vendor contact with no prior relationship; the inbound uses formal business register ('Dear Matt', 'Best regards') confirming this is a new professional outreach.
_autoclassified.service@goosehead.com: LLM classifier high: Goosehead is an insurance service provider with no prior personal relationship indicated — the inbound is an automated reply, no vault entry exists, making this a formal external business contact.
_autoclassified.shannon.scholtens@parkstitle.com: LLM classifier high: Shannon Scholtens at Parks Title is a first-touch external professional contact (title company) being engaged for a formal real estate transaction with no prior relationship on record.
_autoclassified.a vc: LLM classifier high: VC is explicitly listed as 'investors/fund partners' in the Register A definition, and this is a first-touch outbound with no prior thread.

B._label: Mid-Casual — warm biz peers, established relationships

_autoclassified.neal@hypotheticalfounder.io: LLM classifier medium: Neal is a network contact Matt personally introduced to Idan via a warm thread, placing him in the known-ecosystem-peer tier rather than a cold first-touch external, though the lack of direct prior threads with Matt and the mismatch between vault email and outbound address tempers confidence.
_autoclassified.tariq@hypotheticalclient.io: LLM classifier medium: Tariq is a former client with 14 prior meetings and 30 threads, placing him in 'known business contact with prior threads,' though the 9-year gap and career pivot to prop trading make the relationship cold enough to border on A.
_autoclassified.samantha@threadbird.com: LLM classifier high: Sam has replied to an ongoing RFQ thread with a warm, substantive business response, making this a known contact with a prior thread rather than a first-touch external contact.
_autoclassified.steven@rushordertees.com: LLM classifier high: Steven Cruz responded warmly to Matt's RFQ with a first-name greeting and casual tone, establishing an active business thread — this is now an ongoing vendor contact, not a cold first-touch.
_autoclassified.bill@contagiousgraphics.com: LLM classifier high: Bill has replied directly with a warm, practical tone ('Ok, let me know!') establishing an active vendor thread, moving this from first-touch A to ongoing-partner B.
_autoclassified.christan@stweed.com: LLM classifier high: Christan is an account executive at a vendor (Scarborough & Tweed) who has initiated a business thread and signs off 'Best regards' — a known business contact with an active prior thread, fitting mid-casual register.
_autoclassified.hello@baronfig.com: LLM classifier high: Kelley has initiated two prior threads and signed off with her name, establishing a known ongoing vendor relationship that warrants mid-casual register.

C._label: Casual-Pro — close colleagues, peer founders, met-IRL friends

_autoclassified.michael@hypotheticalcofounder.com: LLM classifier high: Michael Chen is an internal Zerg team member (Weekly Sync colleague, ZergAI employee) — maps directly to the C-register 'internal Zerg team' definition.
_autoclassified.michael@zergai.com: LLM classifier high: Michael Chen is an internal Zerg team member (michael@zergai.com) who meets weekly with Matt, placing him squarely in the C-register internal-team category alongside Idan and André.

_excluded._label: Personal/family — DO NOT use professional voice

_autoclassified.sarah@acmevc.example: LLM classifier: Vault CRM identifies Sarah Elizabeth Eisner as family (relationship: Family, topic_tags include Personal), matching the EXCLUDED pattern despite the acmevc.example sender address — the Gmail address seisner1234@gmail.com and family relationship score confirm this is a personal contact, not a professional one.

_fallback_rule: If recipient not in any register: classify by domain + prior-thread tone. Default to B (mid-casual) if unclear. Personal Gmail addresses (gmail.com, hotmail.com) without prior thread → B. Corporate domains without prior thread → A.
